article_title: BIM-Informed Bayesian Post-Hoc Correction for CNN-Based Concrete Defect Classification
key_findings: BIM-informed rule-based correction eliminates 100% of cross-element misclassifications (24→0)
Statistically significant: McNemar p<0.001, Bootstrap ΔAcc CI [+0.0017, +0.0039], P(better)=1.000
Rule-based and constrained BPC are functionally equivalent (99.99% agreement)
Soft Bayesian correction (EN 206) reduces cross-errors by 29% but is not significant for overall accuracy
Method is plug-and-play: no retraining, works with any CNN classifier
BIM mapping scalable to 2000 elements at <20ms (real-time capable)
Remaining 377 errors are intra-element (crack vs no_crack) — not addressable by BIM context
article_contributions: First formalization of BIM-informed Bayesian post-hoc correction for defect classification
Empirical comparison of 6 post-hoc correction strategies on 8420-image test set
Demonstration that deterministic element-constrained correction outperforms soft priors
Error decomposition framework distinguishing cross-element from intra-element errors
Real-time BIM-defect mapping validated up to 2000 elements with 57.5x speedup
